attacking_style: Thrives in chaotic, reduced sprints rather than pure leadout scenarios—launches at 200-350m when positioned wheel-surfing in top 5, exploiting corners and gradients. Increasingly attacks from breakaways (40km+) when sprint form uncertain, targeting intermediate points over stage wins.
team_tactics: BORA orchestrates aggressive tempo on climbs 5-43km from finish to shed pure sprinters, creating reduced bunch sprints that favor his durability. Often freelances without leadout in finales, wheel-surfing rivals (especially at 300-400m) rather than relying on team trains. Team occasionally sacrifices positioning for GC bonuses via domestiques.
terrain_preference: Excels on hilly sprint finishes with 6-12% ramps in final 500m and after multi-climb stages (Cat 2+) that eliminate pure sprinters. Struggles on flat power sprints against organized trains and falters on steep sustained climbs (6%+ for 5km+). Heat tolerance (35°C+) gives advantage over competitors.
vulnerability: Declining explosive speed against younger pure sprinters (BENNETT, GROENEWEGEN, EWAN) in organized finales—frequently boxed, forced lateral, or simply lacks closing watts. Crashes frequently (2021-2023), suggesting reflexes/handling deteriorating. Reputation prevents breakaway cooperation as rivals fear his sprint. Cannot sustain efforts on prolonged technical cobbles (Paris-Roubaix fade pattern).
key_patterns: Wins reduced sprints after team paces climbs hard (5+ observations: Giro 2021, Tour 2018/2019/2020)
Launches 200-350m from finish, never initiating early—matches rivals' timing from 5th-10th wheel (8+ observations)
Beaten in flat organized sprints by younger power sprinters; relegated/boxed frequently 2019-2022 (10+ observations)
Targets intermediate sprint points desperately when stage wins unlikely—breakaway hunting for classification (7+ observations 2020 Tour)
Crash frequency increased 2021-2023: Paris-Roubaix twice, Tour stage wipeouts, suggesting declining technical sharpness
Wins require chaos: disorganized finales, mechanical disruptions to trains, or climbs eliminating sprinters (6+ observations)
form_trajectory: negative: Multiple crashes 2021-2023, 9-month winless drought broken only by chaotic sprints (Tour de Pologne 2022), dropped on moderate climbs (Tour de Suisse 2022), consistently beaten by younger sprinters in organized finales. First win in 15 months (Giro 2020) became pattern requiring perfect circumstances.
